# Problem Statements for Stress Testing

> Auto-generated batch 9 — 50 problems targeting non-obvious tech signals hidden in conversational business language, questions, complaints, and aspirational statements.

## Questions (Posed as Questions, Not Statements)

1. "How do I know if my campaigns are actually working?"

2. "What's the best way to combine data from 20 different systems?"

3. "Can we predict which equipment will fail next?"

4. "Is there a way to get notified when something unusual happens?"

5. "How do we track a product from raw material to customer delivery?"

6. "What's causing our customer churn?"

7. "Can we automate our regulatory filings?"

8. "How much data are we actually using versus just storing?"

9. "Is it possible to let business users explore data without SQL?"

10. "What would it take to process transactions in real-time instead of nightly batches?"

## Complaints (Framed as Frustrations)

11. "Every time we make a decision, someone says the data doesn't support it."

12. "I can't get a straight answer from any of our systems."

13. "We've been waiting months for a simple change to our data pipeline."

14. "The data team says it'll take 6 weeks. The business needs it yesterday."

15. "We hired expensive consultants and all they gave us was a PowerPoint."

16. "Our BI reports take 20 minutes to load and half the time they timeout."

17. "Three different tools show three different versions of our revenue number."

18. "We spent a year building a data platform and then the requirements changed."

19. "Nobody in our organization trusts the data."

20. "Our best analysts keep leaving because they're frustrated with our tools."

## Aspirational (Vision Statements)

21. "We want to become an AI-first company."

22. "Our goal is to democratize data across the entire organization."

23. "We want every decision to be backed by data within 12 months."

24. "We're aiming for zero unplanned downtime on our equipment."

25. "We want to know our customers better than they know themselves."

26. "We want to move from reactive to predictive maintenance."

27. "Our vision is a fully automated supply chain that responds to demand signals in real-time."

28. "We want to create a data marketplace where business units can publish and consume data products."

29. "We want to be compliant by design, not by audit response."

30. "We want our data to tell us what's going to happen, not just what already happened."

## Conversational Scenarios (Natural Dialogue)

31. "So we have these machines, right? And they break down at the worst possible times. Like right in the middle of a big order. Is there any way to know when that's going to happen?"

32. "My team gets these huge CSV files from our vendors every day. Like gigabytes of data. And someone has to manually clean them up before we can do anything with them. It's ridiculous."

33. "We've got all this customer feedback — support tickets, reviews, social media, surveys — and nobody ever looks at it in a holistic way. Each team looks at their own channel."

34. "Here's the thing — we collect a ton of data from our warehouse operations, but the reports are always looking backward. I want to know what's going to happen tomorrow, not what happened yesterday."

35. "Every Friday afternoon, someone runs a query that takes four hours and kills our production database. We need to separate that somehow."

36. "We built this amazing model in a Jupyter notebook and it predicted churn with 92% accuracy. But then what? How do we actually use it in production?"

37. "The CFO keeps asking me how much we spend on data infrastructure. I have no idea because it's spread across seven AWS accounts, three Azure subscriptions, and a Snowflake instance that auto-scales."

38. "Our customers are complaining that personalized recommendations are terrible. We have all the behavioral data but our recommendation engine was built five years ago and hasn't been updated."

39. "We need to merge two companies' worth of customer databases. Same customers might exist in both with different IDs, different spellings, different addresses. It's going to be a nightmare."

40. "One of our data scientists discovered something interesting in the data, but by the time they wrote a report and presented it to management, the market opportunity had passed. We need insights faster."

## Technical-Sounding but Vague

41. "We need a modern data platform."

42. "Our architecture doesn't scale."

43. "We need to do something with AI."

44. "Our data governance needs work."

45. "We need better observability."

46. "Our data mesh initiative stalled."

47. "We need to implement data contracts."

48. "Our feature store doesn't work well."

49. "We need a semantic layer."

50. "Our streaming architecture is unreliable."
